title: Emalee Friend
text:
Research Scientist | Ruminant Nutrition
Welcome to my website!




I am a research scientist at the USDA-ARS Oklahoma and Central Plains Research Center (OCPARC) in Woodward, Oklahoma. I earned my B.S. in Plant and Soil Science, with a minor in Agricultural Finance, from Oklahoma Sate Univeristy. I later completed my M.S. in Ruminant Nutrition at Oklahoma Sate University and am currently persuing my Ph.d. in Ruminant Nutition.



My research centers on improving the energetic efficiency of beef cattle through reducing methane emissions. Also, using cattle from the U.S. Meat Animal Research Center Germplasm Evaluation program, I study how breed type and growth promotants interact to influence performance and carcasses and I am also looking at the effect of dietary protein levels on nutrogen utilization efficiency, growth performance and respiratory gas flux on the different breed types.

title: Emalee’s Webpage
text:
Welcome to my site!
My research focuses on improving the energy efficiency of beef cattle, with an emphasis on reducing methane emissions. I am currently working with cattle from the Germplasm Evaluation Program from the U.S. Meat Animal Research Center. I study how breed type and growth promotants interact to influence performance. I am also interested in how breed differences affect crude protein intake and overall nutrient utilization.



Measuring gas exchange using a GreenFeed
